text: Ba kpaau ki tabinɔ dapunpunn paak gbenn yoonba, ki bi tɔ tiatia ki chent chent u tiat na,
translated: If you want to go to the market, go early in the morning, so that you will find things to buy.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: U din be leŋe nan yoo nba ki Bat Herod din tan kpo na. Linba kur din tun a lin gbeen linba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu din te ki u sɔkiniisɔɔ pak li po nee a “N din nyinn n bija maŋ Ijipt tingbouŋ nie.” Bonjabis kpinu labaar
translated: You did not hear that the king Herod was born. It is said that he was born in Bethlehem, in the days of King Augustus. And all the people were registered in their own towns. So Joseph also went up from the town of Nazareth in Galilee to Bethlehem, the town of David, because he belonged to the house and line of David. He went there to be registered with Mary, his fiancée, who was expecting a child.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Gotir nɔɔnii nba yuuk ki lin na; bi ki buuri; bi bia ki jaan ki wuu suka nan naapaat ni, ŋaan ki i Baa Yennu nba be sanpaapo na dintib. I ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki gar nɔɔnii-ii?
translated: Is it true that you have gone to the market? I went there; I bought some things; I also went to the farm to harvest some yams. I want to ask you, what did you buy?

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N beerie na, barbunu daar, Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan ba din piak baryana kur nba na paak,
translated: If you go there, you will see Yennu, he will give you something to eat and drink, and he will tell you about the things that happened to him.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ŋaan bant man nan li-i tee ki ŋaadaanɔ poŋ mi yoo nba ki nanyukɔɔ yaa wun baar u ŋaak ni nyiɔku, u sii kare ki guu; u kan sak ki nanyukɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ ki jamɔ.
translated: If you want to go to the market, go early in the morning so that you can buy good things; if you want to buy things at the market, go early in the morning so that you can get the best things.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.

translated: You did not see the things that happened in the time of Herod the Great.  You did not hear the things that were said about the birth of Jesus in Egypt.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?

translated: Where are you going? You are going to the market; you are going to buy; you are going to sell your cassava at the market, and you are going to give some to Baa Yennu. Are you going to buy something else?

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N beerie na, barbunu daar, Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan ba din piak baryana kur nba na paak,
translated: If you go there, you will see Yennu, he will give you something to eat and drink, and he will also give you something to take home.

translated: If you go to the market, buy some meat and give it to me, so that I can cook it for you; and also buy some fish for me to fry.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba kpaau ki tabinɔ dapunpunn paak gbenn yoonba, ki bi tɔ tiatia ki chent chent u tiat na,

translated: You did not see the thing that happened to the king Herod.  He was a man who was born in Egypt.  He said to you, “I am the son of God.”  The people were angry.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Gotir nɔɔnii nba yuuk ki lin na; bi ki buuri; bi bia ki jaan ki wuu suka nan naapaat ni, ŋaan ki i Baa Yennu nba be sanpaapo na dintib. I ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki gar nɔɔnii-ii?
translated: Is it true that you have come to this place? If you have come, then you should have come with a good heart; if you have come to do evil, then you should have come with a good heart and not with evil intentions. What is your intention?

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N beerie na, barbunu daar, Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan ba din piak baryana kur nba na paak,
translated: If you are going, go quickly, Yennu, so that you will not be caught by the enemy.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ŋaan bant man nan li-i tee ki ŋaadaanɔ poŋ mi yoo nba ki nanyukɔɔ yaa wun baar u ŋaak ni nyiɔku, u sii kare ki guu; u kan sak ki nanyukɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ ki jamɔ.
translated: If you want to eat, go and look for food, so that you can eat and be satisfied; if you want to drink, go and look for water, so that you can drink and be quenched.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba kpaau ki tabinɔ dapunpunn paak gbenn yoonba, ki bi tɔ tiatia ki chent chent u tiat na,
translated: We have not yet seen the end of the matter, we will see what will happen next.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: U din be leŋe nan yoo nba ki Bat Herod din tan kpo na. Linba kur din tun a lin gbeen linba ki ti Yomdaanɔ Yennu din te ki u sɔkiniisɔɔ pak li po nee a “N din nyinn n bija maŋ Ijipt tingbouŋ nie.” Bonjabis kpinu labaar
translated: You did not see the things that happened in those days. For Herod the king was a man who was afraid of the news from the wise men. When he heard that the wise men had come from the East to worship the king of the Jews, he was afraid. He called all the chief priests and scribes together and asked them, "Where is the Messiah to be born?" They told him, "In Bethlehem in Judea, for this is what the prophet has written: 'And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you will come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.'"

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Gotir nɔɔnii nba yuuk ki lin na; bi ki buuri; bi bia ki jaan ki wuu suka nan naapaat ni, ŋaan ki i Baa Yennu nba be sanpaapo na dintib. I ki mɔk nyɔɔt ki gar nɔɔnii-ii?
translated: Do you know where I am going? I am going to the market; I am going to buy some food; I am going to eat it at home with my family, and I will give some to my father. Do you know where I am going?

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: “N beerie na, barbunu daar, Yennu tan sa bu niib buut nan ba din piak baryana kur nba na paak,
translated: If you go there, you will see Yennu, he is the one who is going to take care of you and give you food.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ŋaan bant man nan li-i tee ki ŋaadaanɔ poŋ mi yoo nba ki nanyukɔɔ yaa wun baar u ŋaak ni nyiɔku, u sii kare ki guu; u kan sak ki nanyukɔɔ-n tan kɔɔ ki jamɔ.
translated: If you want to go to the market, you should go early in the morning so that you can buy things before they are all sold out. You should also be careful not to be cheated.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba kpaau ki tabinɔ dapunpunn paak gbenn yoonba, ki bi tɔ tiatia ki chent chent u tiat na,
translated: If you want to be a good person, you must be honest and truthful in all your dealings.
